title: Classes, Modifiers, Private State, and Abstract Contracts · Spiral Review
summary: Implement scheduling components with runtime invariants, replaceable policies, and explicit construction rules. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a typed scheduling component library after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish instance-side and static-side class types, fields, methods, and constructor signatures.
Use public, protected, TypeScript private, and JavaScript hash-private fields with their actual compile-time and runtime boundaries.
Use parameter properties only when concise construction does not hide validation or ownership transitions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — A scheduling record has id "job-5", an optional owner, readonly creation time, and a mutable status transition. Reject a misspelled status field, protect the invariant, and show which readonly promise exists only at compile time.

Inspect the Composition Inheritance trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose composition or inheritance from substitution, state ownership, variation axes, and test isolation.

content.text: For Composition Inheritance, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Composition Inheritance precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A scheduling record has id "job-5", an optional owner, readonly creation time, and a mutable status transition. Reject a misspelled status field, protect the invariant, and show which readonly promise exists only at compile time.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Typing makes deep inheritance safe by default.

steps.title: Record what required effort
